wine_restaurants.name: L'Assiette Champenoise
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Classic French haute cuisine by Arnaud Lallement; produce-led tasting menus engineered for champagne pairing
wine_restaurants.description: Three-Michelin-star restaurant at Tinqueux run by chef Arnaud Lallement, a family house with a deep champagne cellar. Generous, classics-rooted cooking built.

wine_restaurants.name: Le Parc - Domaine Les Crayeres
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Modern French gastronomy by Christophe Moret; champagne-pairing tasting menus in a chateau dining room
wine_restaurants.description: The two-Michelin-star table of Domaine Les Crayeres, set in a Pommery chateau in Reims. Chef Christophe Moret builds refined menus around a cellar that leans.

wine_restaurants.name: Alcove - Hostellerie La Briqueterie
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Contemporary French cuisine with a champagne-pairing focus inside a Relais and Chateaux hotel
wine_restaurants.description: The Michelin-starred restaurant of Hostellerie La Briqueterie at Vinay, plated for champagne pairing. The cellar runs deep on the Cote des Blancs Chardonnay grown.

wine_restaurants.name: Le Foch
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Seafood-led French gastronomy by Jacky Louaze; menus arranged for champagne and Burgundy pairings
wine_restaurants.description: A one-Michelin-star restaurant on the boulevards of Reims, where chef Jacky Louaze cooks fish and shellfish to order. The list favours house champagnes alongside.

wine_restaurants.name: Restaurant Les Avises
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Single market-led tasting menu built around grand cru Chardonnay and the Selosse champagne range
wine_restaurants.description: The Selosse family restaurant at Les Avises in Avize, where a single daily set menu is plated to flatter grand cru Chardonnay and the domaine champagnes.

wine_restaurants.name: La Grillade Gourmande
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Wood-fired grilled meats and fish plus champagne-cooked specialities by chef Christophe Bernard
wine_restaurants.description: A central Epernay grill where chef-owner Christophe Bernard cooks meats and fish over wood fire, with champagne specialities. The wine list is famously bound like.

wine_restaurants.name: La Cave a Champagne
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Traditional Champenois terroir cooking by Alexandre Ocio; regional dishes served with champagne at table
wine_restaurants.description: A traditional Epernay table founded in 1993 and run by Alexandre Ocio, serving Champenois terroir cooking matched with champagnes by the glass and bottle.

wine_restaurants.name: Le Bocal
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Seafood and shellfish from the adjoining fishmonger, matched to a tight champagne selection
wine_restaurants.description: A tiny 14-seat seafood room behind a fishmonger near the Boulingrin market in Reims. Simple, high-quality shellfish plates paired with a focused list of grower.

wine_restaurants.name: Brasserie Le Jardin - Les Crayeres
wine_restaurants.address: 7 avenue du General Giraud, 51100 Reims, France
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Relaxed French brasserie cooking in the Crayeres park, with a strong champagne by-the-glass program
wine_restaurants.description: The garden brasserie of Domaine Les Crayeres in the Reims park, a relaxed counterpart to Le Parc. French brasserie plates with a generous champagne by-the-glass.

wine_restaurants.name: Le Bistrot du Forum
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Traditional French bistro cooking on the Place du Forum, with a champagne-focused list
wine_restaurants.description: A traditional bistro on the Place du Forum in central Reims, serving classic French plates with a regularly rotating champagne and regional wine selection.
